title: Autism Genomics Atlas - Dataset Catalog Schema
description: Extended schema for dataset entries in the autism genomics atlas, building on the base datasets format

whatYouCanDo.description: Summary of analyses and queries possible with this dataset

whatYouCant.description: Limitations and constraints of the dataset

organism.description: Organism the data is derived from

sampleSize.description: Number of samples in the dataset

primaryUrl.description: Primary web link to the dataset

fileFormats.description: File formats available (VCF, BAM, FASTQ, HDF5, CSV, etc.)

genesRelevant.description: Gene symbols of particular relevance to autism

publicationYear.description: Year of publication or data release

codeSnippet.description: Example code for accessing or analyzing the dataset

lastVerified.description: Timestamp of last verification of dataset access/status

regulatoryRelevance.description: How this dataset relates to noncoding/regulatory variant analysis. Describe functional annotation capacity, enhancer data, promoter elements, etc.

cellTypes.description: Cell types profiled in the dataset (e.g., 'excitatory neurons', 'parvalbumin interneurons', 'astrocytes')

phenotypeData.description: Phenotype information available in the dataset

hasSeverity.description: Whether autism severity/symptom severity is quantified

hasEpilepsyComorbidity.description: Whether epilepsy comorbidity status is recorded

hasIQ.description: Whether IQ or cognitive assessment data is available

description.description: Detailed description of phenotype data structure and availability

variantTypes.description: Types of variants covered in the dataset

atlasIntegrationStatus.description: Current integration status into the atlas

evidenceTier.description: Evidence tier classification (A=highest quality, D=lowest). Null if not applicable.

receptorSeparationRelevance.description: How this dataset relates to glutamate/GABA receptor separation model. Null if not relevant.

backfillPriority.description: Priority level for atlas backfill integration
